title: Temple of Seti I
description: Abydos. New Kingdom (19th Dynasty). Major cult center of Osiris, famous for the Abydos King List and exquisite reliefs.

title: Temple of Ramses II
description: Abydos. New Kingdom (19th Dynasty). Dedicated to Osiris and the deified ancestors, noted for vivid color preservation.

title: The Osireion
description: Abydos. New Kingdom. A subterranean cenotaph structure located behind the Temple of Seti I, meant to resemble the tomb of Osiris.

title: Shunet el-Zebib
description: Abydos. Early Dynastic (2nd Dynasty). A massive mudbrick funerary enclosure built by King Khasekhemwy.

title: Kom el-Sultan
description: Abydos. Early Dynastic to Late Period. The settlement area containing the early Temple of Osiris ruins.

title: Umm el-Qa'ab
description: Abydos. Early Dynastic (1st & 2nd Dynasties). The royal necropolis containing the tombs of Egypt's first pharaohs.

title: Tomb of Djer
description: Abydos (Umm el-Qa'ab). 1st Dynasty. Tomb of the third pharaoh, later revered as the Tomb of Osiris.

title: Tomb of Den
description: Abydos (Umm el-Qa'ab). 1st Dynasty. One of the largest tombs with a granite floor, significant for architectural evolution.

title: Tetisheri Pyramid/Cenotaph
description: South Abydos. New Kingdom (18th Dynasty). A pyramid built by Ahmose I for his grandmother Queen Tetisheri.

title: Pyramid of Ahmose
description: South Abydos. New Kingdom (18th Dynasty). The last royal pyramid built in Egypt, part of Ahmose's mortuary complex.

title: Abydos Boat Graves
description: Abydos. Early Dynastic. A fleet of 14 buried wooden boats discovered near Shunet el-Zebib.

title: Statue of Meritamun
description: Akhmim. New Kingdom (19th Dynasty). A colossal limestone statue of Ramses II's daughter-wife, now in an open-air museum.

title: Temple of Min Ruins
description: Akhmim. Greco-Roman Period. Scanty remains of a once-massive temple dedicated to the fertility god Min.

title: El-Hawawish
description: Akhmim (East Bank). Old Kingdom to Late Period. The main necropolis of the ancient city of Panopolis (Akhmim).

title: El-Salamuni
description: Akhmim (East Bank). Greco-Roman. Features rock-cut tombs, a temple of Min built by Ay, and Coptic paintings.

title: Temple of Triphis
description: Athribis (Wannina). Ptolemaic (Ptolemy XII). A large temple dedicated to the lioness goddess Triphis (Repyt).

title: Roman Scenic Tomb
description: Athribis (Wannina). Roman Period. A tomb featuring zodiac ceilings and detailed colorful astrological scenes.

title: White Monastery (Deir al-Abyad)
description: Sohag (West Bank). Coptic (5th Century AD). Established by St. Shenouda, built with white limestone from Pharaonic temples.

title: Red Monastery (Deir al-Ahmar)
description: Sohag (West Bank). Coptic (4th Century AD). Famous for its church sanctuary featuring exceptionally preserved ancient frescoes.

title: Sidi Arif Mosque
description: Sohag City. Islamic (Mamluk/Ottoman style). One of the oldest and most significant mosques in the city, known for its distinct minarets.

title: Al-Atiq Mosque (Al-Farshuti)
description: Sohag City. Islamic (Fatimid origins). Also known as the Old Mosque, it holds historical significance in the region.

title: Sidi Galal Mosque (Porcelain Mosque)
description: Sohag City. Islamic. Known for its intricate ceramic/porcelain decoration (Qishani)..

title: Masjid Al-Amir Hassan
description: Akhmim. Islamic (Ottoman Era). Contains the mausoleum of Prince Hassan, known for its fine woodwork and dome.

title: Beit Khallaf Mastaba K1
description: Beit Khallaf. 3rd Dynasty. A massive mudbrick mastaba attributed to Djoser or Sanakht.

title: Beit Khallaf Mastaba K2
description: Beit Khallaf. 3rd Dynasty. Another large mudbrick mastaba in the same necropolis.

title: El-Hagarsa
description: West of Sohag. Old Kingdom to First Intermediate Period. A necropolis with rock-cut tombs of local governors.

title: Gebel al-Haridi
description: East Bank (Nazlet el-Haridi). Coptic/Pharaonic. Features quarries, Ptolemaic inscriptions, and ancient Coptic hermitages.

title: Kom Ishqaw (Aphrodito)
description: Tama District. Coptic/Byzantine. Ancient city of Aphrodito, famous for the discovery of the Aphrodito Papyri.

title: Sohag National Museum
description: Sohag City. Modern. Houses thousands of artifacts from the governorate, including items from Abydos and Akhmim.

title: Deir el-Malak (Monastery of the Angel)
description: Akhmim (East of city). Coptic. Dedicated to Archangel Michael, located in a desert area near El-Salamuni.

title: Deir al-Shuhada (Monastery of Martyrs)
description: Akhmim. Coptic. Dedicated to the martyrs of the Diocletianic Persecution.

title: Deir Anba Bisada
description: Opposite Sohag (East Bank). Coptic. An ancient monastery featuring distinct Coptic architecture.

title: Deir Mar Girgis (Iron)
description: Akhmim. Coptic. The Monastery of St. George, known locally as Deir Al-Hadid.

title: Deir El-Adhra (Virgin Mary)
description: Akhmim (El-Hawawish area). Coptic. Located high on the mountain near the necropolis.

title: Deir Anba Thomas
description: Near Akhmim (Shinera). Coptic. Dedicated to St. Thomas the Hermit.

title: Naga ed-Deir
description: Opposite Girga (East Bank). Predynastic to Middle Kingdom. A vast cemetery site yielding significant archaeological finds.

title: El-Mahasna
description: North of Abydos. Predynastic. A significant settlement and cemetery site from the Naqada period.

title: Reqaqnah
description: West of Gerga. 3rd Dynasty. Necropolis containing staircase mastabas.

title: El-Mashayikh (Lepidotonpolis)
description: East Bank (Opposite Girga). New Kingdom. Site of a rock-cut temple built by Amenhotep III.

title: El-Amra
description: South of Abydos. Predynastic (Naqada). Type-site for the Amratian culture.

title: Wannina Temple Enclosure
description: Athribis (Wannina). Greco-Roman. The broader archaeological area surrounding the Temple of Triphis.

title: Chapel of Ay
description: El-Salamuni (Akhmim). New Kingdom (18th Dynasty). A rock-cut shrine dedicated to Min by King Ay.

title: Al-Kawamil
description: West Bank (South of Sohag). Predynastic. A major settlement and cemetery site.

title: Tomb of Khasekhemwy
description: Abydos (Umm el-Qa'ab). 2nd Dynasty. The last and largest tomb in the royal cemetery.

title: Coptic Church of St. Demiana
description: Akhmim. Coptic. A historic church revered in the local tradition.

title: Mosque of Sheikh Mursi
description: Sohag City. Islamic. A notable local mosque and religious landmark.

title: Tomb of Peribsen
description: Abydos (Umm el-Qa'ab). 2nd Dynasty. Tomb of the Seth-worshipping king, located north of Khasekhemwy.

title: Askala Temple (Athribis)
description: Athribis (Wannina). Ptolemaic (Ptolemy IX). A smaller temple dedicated to the child god Kolanthes.
